subject: Geography
title: Antarctica: The Frozen Continent
description: A quick, practical guide to Antarctica: The Frozen Continent.

hook.text: Antarctica holds 70% of the world's fresh water—all locked up as ice.

buildup.text: It is the coldest, driest, windiest, and highest continent on Earth.

discovery.text: The interior receives less than 50 mm of precipitation per year—technically a desert.

twist.text: Beneath the ice, scientists have found over 400 subglacial lakes, including one the size of Ontario.

climax.text: Antarctica is a desert on top but holds an ocean of hidden water below its ice sheet.

punchline.text: The driest continent hides the most water. It's all frozen.

quiz.question: Why is Antarctica technically a desert?
options: It receives less than 50 mm of precipitation per year
It has sand dunes under the ice
